# langfuse_llm_connection (Resource)

Manages a Langfuse LLM connection for playground and evaluation features.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "langfuse_llm_connection" "openai" {
  name                = "openai-production"
  adapter             = "openai"
  api_key             = var.openai_api_key
  with_default_models = true
}

resource "langfuse_llm_connection" "azure" {
  name     = "azure-openai"
  adapter  = "azure"
  base_url = "https://my-resource.openai.azure.com"
  api_key  = var.azure_api_key
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `adapter` (String) The LLM adapter / provider (e.g. `openai`, `anthropic`). Maps to the Langfuse `provider` field. Named `adapter` because `provider` is a reserved attribute name in Terraform.
- `name` (String) The name of the LLM connection.

### Optional

- `api_key` (String, Sensitive) The API key for the LLM provider. This is write-only and will not be read back from the API.
- `base_url` (String) The base URL for the LLM provider API.
- `with_default_models` (Boolean) Whether to include default models for this provider.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the LLM connection.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
# Import by connection name (used as identifier since the API has no Get-by-ID endpoint)
terraform import langfuse_llm_connection.openai openai-production
```
